title: UPSC CDS 2026
meta_description: UPSC CDS 2026 — Combined Defence Services exam for graduates to join Army (IMA), Navy, Air Force, and OTA as officers. Exam pattern, salary, SSB interview, and eligibility.

hero.one_liner: Officer entry into Indian Armed Forces for graduates — join IMA, Naval Academy, Air Force Academy, or Officers Training Academy through UPSC

what_is_it: The Combined Defence Services (CDS) examination is conducted by UPSC twice a year to recruit graduates for officer-level entry into the Indian Military Academy (IMA — Army), Naval Academy, Air Force Academy, and Officers Training Academy (OTA). It's the primary path for graduates to become military officers without going through NDA (which is for 12th pass candidates).

CDS has two entries annually — CDS I (exam in April, course starts in January next year) and CDS II (exam in September, course starts in July next year). The selection has two stages: Written Exam (UPSC) and SSB Interview (5-day personality assessment). The written exam tests English, General Knowledge, and Elementary Mathematics.

IMA and OTA entries are open to graduates of any stream. Naval Academy requires Engineering degree. Air Force Academy requires degree with Physics + Mathematics at 12th level or BE/BTech. OTA offers Short Service Commission (SSC) — 14 years of service — while IMA, Naval Academy, and AFA offer Permanent Commission.

After selection, cadets undergo training at the respective academy: IMA Dehradun (18 months), Naval Academy (various courses), AFA Dundigal (74 weeks), or OTA Chennai (49 weeks). Upon commissioning, officers start at the rank of Lieutenant with basic pay of ₹56,100/month.

key_details.label: Education
key_details.value: IMA/OTA: Any graduation. Naval Academy: Engineering. AFA: Graduation with Physics+Maths at 12th level or BE/BTech.

key_details.label: Age Limit
key_details.value: IMA/AFA: 20-25 years. Naval Academy: 19-25 years. OTA: 20-27 years.

key_details.label: Gender
key_details.value: IMA/Naval/AFA: Men only. OTA: Men AND Women.

key_details.label: Selection
key_details.value: Written Exam → SSB Interview (5 days) → Medical → Merit

exam_pattern.tier_name: Written Exam — IMA, AFA, Naval Academy (3 papers)
exam_pattern.description: Three papers of 2 hours each: English (100 marks), General Knowledge (100 marks), and Elementary Mathematics (100 marks). Total 300 marks. OTA candidates write only English + GK (200 marks, no Maths).

extra_sections.heading: IMA vs OTA — Permanent vs Short Service Commission
rows: Military experience + option to return to civilian life

extra_sections.heading: CDS vs NDA vs Agnipath
content: CDS is for graduates (20-27 years). NDA is for 12th pass (16.5-19.5 years). Agnipath is for 10th/12th pass (17.5-23 years) but as enlisted personnel, not officers.

If you missed NDA during 12th, CDS is your second chance to become a military officer. CDS written exam is easier than NDA (no advanced mathematics), but the SSB interview is the same challenging 5-day assessment.

Key advantage of CDS over NDA: You already have a degree, so training is shorter (18 months IMA vs 3+1 years NDA). Key advantage of NDA over CDS: NDA graduates are considered senior to CDS-entry officers of the same batch and get a free degree during training.
Both NDA and CDS lead to the same officer rank and pay. After a few years of service, there's no practical difference between NDA-entry and CDS-entry officers.

preparation_tips: CDS English paper tests grammar, vocabulary, comprehension, and sentence structure. It's more language-focused than NDA. Practice: sentence correction, synonyms/antonyms, idioms, fill in blanks, reading comprehension, and paragraph ordering. Wren & Martin is the go-to book.

General Knowledge is broad — history, geography, science, economics, polity, and current affairs. Focus on NCERT 6-12 for static GK. For current affairs, 6 months of newspaper reading + a monthly magazine is sufficient.

Mathematics is 'Elementary' — up to 10th class level. Much easier than NDA math. Don't over-prepare this section. Focus on arithmetic, algebra, trigonometry, geometry, mensuration, and statistics at basic levels.

SSB interview preparation is critical — start from Day 1. Read about Officer Like Qualities (OLQs), practice group discussions, develop opinions on national issues, and work on physical fitness. SSB is where most candidates are eliminated.

faqs.question: Can women apply for CDS?
faqs.answer: Women can apply for OTA (Officers Training Academy) entry through CDS. IMA, Naval Academy, and Air Force Academy entries through CDS are currently for men only. Women can join the Air Force through the AFCAT exam and Navy through specific entries — check respective service websites.

faqs.question: I have a commerce/arts degree. Can I join the Army?
faqs.answer: Yes. IMA and OTA accept graduates of ANY stream — Arts, Commerce, Science, Engineering, anything. Only Naval Academy requires Engineering degree, and AFA requires Physics+Maths at 12th level. An Arts graduate can become an Army officer through CDS-IMA or CDS-OTA.

faqs.question: What is the CDS written exam cutoff?
faqs.answer: CDS cutoffs vary by year and entry. Typically: IMA — 110-130 out of 300, OTA — 90-110 out of 200. These are relatively low — the real challenge is the SSB interview. Focus on clearing the cutoff with a comfortable margin, then dedicate maximum effort to SSB preparation.

faqs.question: Short Service Commission — can I leave after 14 years?
faqs.answer: Yes. SSC officers serve for 14 years with an option to extend. They can also apply for Permanent Commission conversion (subject to vacancies and performance). After leaving, SSC officers receive gratuity and resettlement benefits. Many SSC officers transition to corporate careers, civil services, or entrepreneurship after military service.

faqs.question: Do I need to be physically fit for CDS?
faqs.answer: Yes. You must pass the SSB physical tasks (obstacle course, outdoor group tasks) and a thorough military medical examination. Vision: 6/6 in better eye, 6/9 in worse eye (with correction allowed for some entries). No color blindness. Weight proportionate to height. Start physical fitness training 6 months before the exam.
